_draft_note: MISFIRE-EXEMPLAR 2026-05-17 — DO NOT PROMOTE. Off-target on two counts: (a) wrong beat — 'Beat 1 Murrow' was a typo for Beat 4 per Piotr 2026-05-17; (b) violates the no-new-flag rule (introduces chapter1.murrow_pre_pig_dwell). Retained as a structural exemplar for the Ch1 sweep prompt template only; the actual Murrow Beat 4 draft will live in beat4_murrow_2026-05-17.json. Original note follows. INERT: lives under data/_drafts/, date-suffixed, never loaded by dialogue_runner.gd. Canon mismatch flagged: story.txt §Beat 1 ('Office Street arrival, pure mechanical, no NPC interaction') and narrative_revision/beats/chapter_1.md §Beat 1 ('(K) all. Pure mechanical. No NPC interaction.') both place Murrow's first speaking beat at Beat 4 (eviction briefing + first-name calibration), already authored in godot/data/dialogues/murrow.json::murrow_first_meeting. Beat 1 has no Murrow content by canon. Interpreting the request as 'Beat 1/early-Beat-2 window before Cula meets Mr. Pig' — i.e. the window where Murrow is canonically 'not immediately available or partly hidden near the file area' (story.txt L174). States authored here are pre-met-Pig, pre-met-Murrow ambient/proximity behavior, richer than the existing single before_pig state in murrow.json. All state ids prefixed murrow_b1_ to avoid collision with the canonical file. If promoted, the dwell flag below must be added to State.reset_state() and the existing before_pig state should be retired or this draft's pivot state inlined in its place.
_beat_ref: story.txt §Beat 1 (lines 117-145); narrative_revision/beats/chapter_1.md §Beat 1 (line 73-75); secondary: story.txt §Beat 2 lines 172-176 (Murrow 'not immediately available or is partly hidden near the file area, forcing the player to look around').
_flag_declaration_notes: chapter1.met_pig and chapter1.met_murrow are existing canonical flags. chapter1.murrow_pre_pig_dwell is a NEW string flag this draft introduces (values: 'work' | 'office' | 'pig' | null). If this file is promoted, declare default null in State.reset_state() under chapter1, bump SAVE_VERSION, add migration step, add migration test — per godot/AGENTS.md §Save migration policy. Boot validator would reject the write_path until the declaration lands.

states._comment: Source: story.txt §Beat 2 L174 ('Murrow is not immediately available or is partly hidden near the file area, forcing the player to look around'); narrative_revision/beats/chapter_1.md §Beat 1 ('pure mechanical, no NPC interaction'). Voice anchor: voice_references/dialogue_samples_mr_murrow.jsonl murrow_ch01_chapter1_001 (file-fact register, dry redirect), murrow_global_global_011 (Mr. Pig framed without cruelty), and rich anchors murrow_global_voice_anchors_002 ('The file is not difficult. It is incomplete.'), 037 ('Mr. Pig has begun in his usual key'). Address-form rule: AGENTS.md §Address forms (Cula → 'Mr. Murrow' before friend-invitation; Murrow → 'Doctor Cula' to Cula until later beats — matches existing before_pig and murrow_first_meeting in canonical murrow.json). No friend-invitation here; that lands in Beat 4 (murrow_first_meeting). Lines cap: 4 Murrow + 1 Cula interject, well under the 8-NPC-line ceiling. Jokes: 'a noisy day to begin' (voice-pack anchor, dry compression) + 'panic is not a pleading' (rich anchor 004) reframed as referral to Pig. Single archival adjective load-bearing: 'noisy' (the only adjective in this state's Murrow lines).
lines: Doctor Cula. You have picked a noisy day to begin.
Mr. Pig is expecting you. Whatever he has, it has been waiting since Tuesday.
Read Mr. Pig first. He will not stay in sequence if you reverse the order. The file will keep.

choices.text: Anything I should know about him before I go over?
I should go find Mr. Pig.

states._comment: Source: voice_agents/murrow.md §With Mr. Pig ('long-married rhythm; finds Pig genuinely amusing in a way he would never admit to Pig'); voice_agents/murrow.md §Patterns ('The framed reassurance' and 'The protective course-correction'). The line is the protective gesture — Murrow telling Cula how to navigate the room, dressed as institutional observation. ai_voice_constraints.md §Murrow Will-not-say: no reassurance, no cruelty about Pig. Compliance: 'in good faith' frames panic without softening it; no 'don't worry'. Sincere-line ration: zero sincere lines (this is dry framing). Address-form rule: 'Doctor Cula' preserved; Cula does not need to address Murrow again mid-chain. Joke: 'in good faith, which is a separate inquiry from accurately' (Mrożek-flavored fiction-naming move per murrow.md §Naming the fiction). Single adjective load-bearing: 'composed' (used to set up the deflation in line 2). Chain: false — returns Cula to the room to commit to Pig, the player-visible change is the player walking to Pig with framed expectations. Once: false (dwell may re-fire on second pass if player walks away without meeting Pig).
lines: Mr. Pig has been with the firm longer than most of its furniture.
He begins composed and finishes underwater. He is panicking in good faith, which is a separate inquiry from accurately.
Hear him out. The case in his mouth will be a weather report. The case in the file is narrower. We will arrive at the file together.

states._comment: Source: voice_agents/murrow.md §How Murrow talks (cite + ironic gloss + directive; archive holds, Murrow remembers); voice references murrow_global_voice_anchors_025 ('On file. Not, unfortunately, in order.'). The state surfaces the eviction case obliquely without naming Halina or the address mechanism — both belong to the canonical Beat 4 briefing in murrow_first_meeting and must not pre-fire here. Murrow names the procedural shape of his current work ('eviction notice that arrived after the hearing') as a teaser; Cula does not yet have the binder, the case content, or the client. ai_voice_constraints.md §Murrow Will-not-say compliance: no reassurance, no editorial; the line is observational. Address-form: 'Doctor Cula' preserved. Future-proofing (Taste Standard 5): the 'envelope arrived after the hearing' line is consistent with the canonical wrong-address mechanism (2015 renumbering, ex parte hearing) but does not commit to specifics — the briefing in Beat 4 remains the load-bearing reveal. Jokes: 'envelope arrived after the hearing it was meant to warn about' (procedural irony per murrow.md §Naming the fiction) + 'in alphabetical order, which is the kindest thing the file does for them' (dry compression, Mrożek-adjacent). Single adjective load-bearing: 'kindest' (Murrow's adjective economy holds; it's doing the irony). on_dismiss clears the dwell flag so further E-presses re-fire the first-sighting state's option block.
lines: A motion. The envelope arrived after the hearing it was meant to warn about.
The dates in the file are in alphabetical order, which is the kindest thing the file does for them.
I will brief you on it after you have spoken with Mr. Pig. He will give you the panic. I will give you the chronology.

states._comment: Fallback for second-or-later E-press in the same window after murrow_b1_first_sighting has fired (once:true on that state means it does not re-fire). This state catches the bare re-approach without the option block. Source: voice_agents/murrow.md §Default move when uncertain ('have him produce a short framing of what is actually happening, attach a procedural fact, end with a directive'); voice references murrow_global_global_005 ('A second conversation rarely changes the law'). Address-form: 'Doctor Cula' preserved. Line cap: 2 Murrow lines, no Cula interject required — the line is a redirect, not a scene. Joke: 'the file is not new since the last time you read it' (dry compression, archive-rule expression). The directive points back to Pig — single Murrow scene, the player's visible next action is unchanged. No options. on_dismiss empty (no state mutation; the redirect is the entire effect). Trigger note: this state appears AFTER murrow_b1_first_sighting in JSON order so the once-state wins priority on first fire; the runner walks top-to-bottom, so murrow_b1_first_sighting must precede this state in the array — see godot/data/dialogues/_schema.md §States.
lines: The file is not newer than it was a moment ago.
Mr. Pig, then the briefing. I will be at this shelf.

idle_flavor: I am locating an envelope. The envelope is locating itself, less helpfully.
The shelf is in order. The chronology, separately, is not.
On file. Not, unfortunately, in any sequence the court will recognise.
